title: trifolium ludovicianum
intro: Trifolium ludovicianum, also known as prairie clover, is a perennial legume native to North America, characterized by its pink or purple flowers and trifoliate leaves. The medicinal properties of prairie clover include its high levels of isoflavones, saponins, and phenolic acids, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of prairie clover include its potential to help manage menopausal symptoms, reduce inflammation, and support cardiovascular health. Prairie clover is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or dried herb for its medicinal and culinary purposes.
